sections.label: Section 3.460
sections.original: μέριμνα νυκτηρεφές .
sections.translation: In anxious fear I wait to hear something shrouded still in gloom . The gods are not blind to men with blood upon their hands . In the end the black Spirits of Vengeance bring to obscurity that one who has prospered in unrighteousness and

sections.label: Section 3.465
sections.original: παλιντυχεῖ [*] τριβᾷ βίου
sections.translation: wear down his fortunes by reverse . Once a man is among the unseen , there is no more help for him . Glory in excess is fraught with peril ;

sections.label: Section 3.470
sections.original: σοις Διόθεν κεραυνός .
sections.translation: the lofty peak is struck by Zeus ’ thunderbolt . I choose prosperity unassailed by envy . May I not be a sacker of cities , and may I not myself be despoiled and live to see my own life in another ’ s power !

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: πυρὸς δ ὑπ εὐαγγέλου [*]
sections.translation: Heralded by a beacon of good tidings a swift report has spread throughout the town . Yet whether it is true , or some deception of the gods , who knows ?

sections.label: Section 479
sections.original: τίς ὧδε παιδνὸς ἢ φρενῶν κεκομμένος ,
sections.translation: Who is so childish or so bereft of sense ,

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: φλογὸς παραγγέλμασιν
sections.translation: once he has let his heart be fired by sudden news of a beacon fire , to despair if the story changes ?

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: [ ἐν ] [*] γυναικὸς αἰχμᾷ πρέπει
sections.translation: It is just like a woman ’ s eager nature to yield assent to pleasing news before yet the truth is clear .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: πιθανὸς ἄγαν ὁ θῆλυς ὅρος [*] ἐπινέμεται
sections.translation: Too credulous , a woman ’ s mind has boundaries open to quick encroachment ; but quick to perish is rumor spread by a woman .

sections.label: Section 488
sections.original: τάχ εἰσόμεσθα λαμπάδων φαεσφόρων [*]
sections.translation: We shall soon know about this passing on of flaming lights

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: φρυκτωριῶν τε καὶ πυρὸς παραλλαγάς ,
sections.translation: and beacon signals and fires , whether they perhaps are true or whether , dream-like , this light ’ s glad coming has beguiled our senses . Look ! I see approaching from the shore a herald crowned with boughs of olive .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: πηλοῦ ξύνουρος διψία κόνις τάδε ,
sections.translation: The thirsty dust , consorting sister of the mud [*] , assures me that neither by pantomime nor by kindling a flame of mountain wood will he signal with smoke of fire . Either in plain words he will bid us to rejoice the more , or — but I have little love for the report opposite to this !

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: εὖ γὰρ πρὸς εὖ φανεῖσι προσθήκη πέλοι .—
sections.translation: May still further good be added to the good that has appeared !

sections.label: Section 501
sections.original: ὅστις τάδ ἄλλως τῇδ ἐπεύχεται πόλει ,
sections.translation: Whoever makes this prayer with other intent toward the state , let him reap himself the fruit of his misguided purpose !

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: ἰὼ πατρῷον οὖδας Ἀργείας χθονός ,
sections.translation: All hail , soil of Argos , land of my fathers ! On this happy day in the tenth year I have come to you .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: πολλῶν ῥαγεισῶν ἐλπίδων μιᾶς τυχών .
sections.translation: Many hopes have shattered , one only have I seen fulfilled ; for I never dared to dream that here in this land of Argos I should die and have due portion of burial most dear to me . Now blessings on the land , blessings on the light of the sun , and blessed be Zeus , the land ’ s Most High , and the Pythian lord ;

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: τόξοις ἰάπτων μηκέτ εἰς ἡμᾶς βέλη :
sections.translation: and may he launch no more his shafts against us . Enough of your hostility did you display by Scamander ’ s banks ; but now , in other mood , be our preserver and our healer , O lord Apollo . And the gods gathered here , I greet them all ; him , too , my own patron ,

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: Ἑρμῆν , φίλον κήρυκα , κηρύκων σέβας ,
sections.translation: Hermes , beloved herald , of heralds all revered ; and the heroes [*] who sped us forth , I pray that they may receive back in kindliness the remnant of the host which has escaped the spear . Hail , halls of our kings , beloved roofs , and you august seats , and you divinities that face the sun [*] ,

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: εἴ που [*] πάλαι , φαιδροῖσι τοισίδ ὄμμασι
sections.translation: if ever you did in days gone by , now after long lapse of years , with gladness in your eyes receive your king . For bearing light in darkness to you and to all assembled here alike , he has returned — Agamemnon , our king . Oh greet him well , as is right ,

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: Τροίαν κατασκάψαντα τοῦ δικηφόρου
sections.translation: since he has uprooted Troy with the mattock of Zeus the Avenger , with which her soil has been uptorn . Demolished are the altars and the shrines of her gods ; and the seed of her whole land has been wasted utterly . Upon the neck of Troy he has cast such a yoke .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ἄναξ Ἀτρείδης πρέσβυς εὐδαίμων ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: Now he has come home , our king , Atreus ’ elder son , a man of happy fate , worthy of honor beyond all living men . For neither Paris nor his partner city can boast that the deed was greater than the suffering . Convicted for robbery and for theft as well ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: τοῦ ῥυσίου θ ἥμαρτε καὶ πανώλεθρον
sections.translation: he has lost the plunder and has razed in utter destruction his father ’ s house and even the land . The sons of Priam have paid a twofold penalty for their sins .

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: κῆρυξ Ἀχαιῶν χαῖρε τῶν ἀπὸ στρατοῦ .
sections.translation: Joy to you , Herald from the Achaean host !

sections.label: Section 539
sections.original: χαίρω γε : [*] τεθνάναι δ οὐκέτ [*] ἀντερῶ θεοῖς .
sections.translation: I do rejoice . I will no longer refuse to die , if that pleases the gods .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἔρως πατρῴας τῆσδε γῆς σ ἐγύμνασεν .
sections.translation: Was it yearning for this your fatherland that wore you out ?

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: ὥστ ἐνδακρύειν [*] γ ὄμμασιν χαρᾶς ὕπο .
sections.translation: Yes , so that my eyes are filled with tears for joy .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: τερπνῆς ἄρ ἦτε τῆσδ ἐπήβολοι νόσου . [*]
sections.translation: It was then a pleasing malady from which you suffered .

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: πῶς δή ; διδαχθεὶς τοῦδε δεσπόσω λόγου .
sections.translation: How so ? Teach me , and I shall master what you say .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: τῶν ἀντερώντων ἱμέρῳ πεπληγμένοι . [*]
sections.translation: You were smitten with desire for those who returned your love .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: ποθεῖν ποθοῦντα τήνδε γῆν στρατὸν λέγεις .
sections.translation: Do you mean that our land longed for the longing host ?

sections.label: Section 546
sections.original: ὡς πόλλ ἀμαυρᾶς ἐκ φρενός μ [*] ἀναστένειν .
sections.translation: Longed so , that often from a darkly brooding spirit I have sighed .

sections.label: Section 547
sections.original: πόθεν τὸ δύσφρον τοῦτ ἐπῆν στύγος φρενῶν ; [*]
sections.translation: Where did this gloom of melancholy upon your spirit come from ?

sections.label: Section 548
sections.original: πάλαι τὸ σιγᾶν φάρμακον βλάβης ἔχω .
sections.translation: Long since have I found silence an antidote to harm .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ; ἀπόντων κοιράνων ἔτρεις τινάς ;
sections.translation: How so ? Did you fear anyone when our princes were gone ?

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: ὡς [*] νῦν τὸ σὸν δή , καὶ θανεῖν πολλὴ χάρις .
sections.translation: In such fear that now , in your own words , even death would be great joy .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: εὖ γὰρ πέπρακται . ταῦτα δ ἐν πολλῷ χρόνῳ
sections.translation: Yes , all ’ s well , well ended . Yet , of what occurred in the long years , one might well say that part fell out happily , and part in turn amiss . But who , unless he is a god , is free from suffering all his days ?

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: μόχθους γὰρ εἰ λέγοιμι καὶ δυσαυλίας ,
sections.translation: For were I to recount our hardships and our wretched quarters , the scanty space and the sorry berths —— what did we not have to complain of . . . [*] Then again , ashore , there was still worse to loathe ; for we had to lie down close to the enemy ’ s walls ,

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ἐξ οὐρανοῦ δὲ [*] κἀπὸ γῆς λειμώνιαι
sections.translation: and the drizzling from the sky and the dews from the meadows distilled upon us , working constant destruction to our clothes and filling our hair with vermin . And if one were to tell of the wintry cold , past all enduring , when Ida ’ s snow slew the birds ;

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ἢ θάλπος , εὖτε πόντος ἐν μεσημβριναῖς
sections.translation: or of the heat , when upon his waveless noonday couch , windless the sea sank to sleep — but why should we bewail all this ? Our labor ’ s past ; past for the dead so that they will never care even to wake to life again .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: τί τοὺς ἀναλωθέντας ἐν ψήφῳ λέγειν ,
sections.translation: Why should we count the number of the slain , or why should the living feel pain at their past harsh fortunes ? Our misfortunes should , in my opinion , bid us a long farewell . For us , the remnant of the Argive host , the gain has the advantage and the loss does not bear down the scale ;

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: ὡς κομπάσαι τῷδ εἰκὸς ἡλίου φάει
sections.translation: so that , as we speed over land and sea , it is fitting that we on this bright day make this boast : [*] The Argive army , having taken Troy at last , has nailed up these spoils to be a glory for the gods throughout Hellas in their shrines from days of old .

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: τοιαῦτα χρὴ κλύοντας εὐλογεῖν πόλιν
sections.translation: Whoever hears the story of these deeds must extol the city and the leaders of her host ; and the grace of Zeus that brought them to accomplishment shall receive its due measure of gratitude . There , you have heard all that I have to say .

sections.label: Section 583
sections.original: νικώμενος λόγοισιν οὐκ ἀναίνομαι :
sections.translation: Your words have proved me wrong . I do not deny it ; for the old have ever enough youth to learn aright .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: δόμοις δὲ ταῦτα καὶ Κλυταιμήστρᾳ μέλειν
sections.translation: But these tidings should have most interest for the household and Clytaemestra , and at the same time enrich me .

sections.label: Section 587
sections.original: ἀνωλόλυξα μὲν πάλαι χαρᾶς ὕπο ,
sections.translation: I raised a shout of triumph in my joy long before this , when the first flaming messenger arrived by night , telling that Ilium was captured and overthrown .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: καί τίς μ ἐνίπτων εἶπε , “ φρυκτωρῶν δία
sections.translation: Then there were some who chided me and said : Are you so convinced by beacon-fires as to think that Troy has now been sacked ? Truly , it is just like a woman to be elated in heart . By such taunts I was made to seem as if my wits were wandering . Nevertheless I still held on with my sacrifice , and throughout all the quarters of the city , according to their womanly custom ,

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: ὀλολυγμὸν ἄλλος ἄλλοθεν κατὰ πτόλιν
sections.translation:
they raised a shout of happy praise while in the shrines of the gods they lulled to rest the fragrant spice-fed flame .
So now why should you rehearse to me the account at length ? From the king himself I shall hear the whole tale ;

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: ὅπως δ ἄριστα τὸν ἐμὸν αἰδοῖον πόσιν
sections.translation: but I should hasten to welcome my honored husband best on his return . For what joy is sweeter in a woman ’ s eyes than to unbar the gates for her husband when God has spared him to return from war ? Give this message to my husband :

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ἥκειν ὅπως τάχιστ ἐράσμιον πόλει :
sections.translation: let him come with all speed , his country ’ s fond desire , come to find at home his wife faithful , even as he left her , a watchdog of his house , loyal to him , a foe to those who wish him ill ; yes , for the rest , unchanged in every part ;

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: οὐδὲν διαφθείρασαν ἐν μήκει χρόνου .
sections.translation: in all this length of time never having broken any seal . Of pleasure from any other man or of scandalous repute I know no more than of dyeing bronze . Exit

sections.label: Section 613
sections.original: τοιόσδ ὁ κόμπος τῆς ἀληθείας γέμων [*]
sections.translation: A boast like this , loaded full with truth , does not shame the speech of a noble wife .

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: αὕτη μὲν οὕτως εἶπε μανθάνοντί σοι
sections.translation: Thus has she spoken for your schooling , but speciously for those that can interpret right . But , Herald , say — I want to hear of Menelaus . Has he , our land ’ s dear lord , travelled safe home and has he returned with you ?

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσθ ὅπως λέξαιμι τὰ ψευδῆ καλὰ
sections.translation: It would be impossible to report false news so fair that those I love should take pleasure for long .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: πῶς δῆτ ἂν εἰπὼν κεδνὰ τἀληθῆ τύχοις ; [*] [*]
sections.translation: Oh if only you could tell tidings true yet good ! It is not easy to conceal when true and good are split apart .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: ἁνὴρ [*] ἄφαντος ἐξ Ἀχαιικοῦ στρατοῦ ,
sections.translation: The prince was swept from the sight of the Achaean host ,

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: αὐτός τε καὶ τὸ πλοῖον . οὐ ψευδῆ λέγω .
sections.translation: himself , and his ship likewise . I speak no lies .

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: πότερον ἀναχθεὶς ἐμφανῶς ἐξ Ἰλίου ,
sections.translation: Did he put forth in sight of all from Ilium , or did a storm , distressing all in common , snatch him from the fleet ?

sections.label: Section 628
sections.original: ἔκυρσας ὥστε τοξότης ἄκρος σκοποῦ :
sections.translation: Like a master bowman you have hit the mark ; a long tale of distress have you told in brief .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: πότερα γὰρ αὐτοῦ ζῶντος ἢ τεθνηκότος
sections.translation: Did the general voice of other voyagers bring news of him as alive or dead ?

sections.label: Section 632
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδεν οὐδεὶς ὥστ ἀπαγγεῖλαι τορῶς ,
sections.translation: None knows to give clear report of this — except only the Sun that fosters life upon the earth .

sections.label: Section 634
sections.original: πῶς γὰρ λέγεις χειμῶνα ναυτικῷ στρατῷ
sections.translation: How then do you say
